name: Port de Catarroja Rice Fields Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de l'Albufera — Circular desde el Port de Catarroja
description: Enjoy an easy 3.1-mile circular hike through the rice fields and wetlands of Parque Natural de l'Albufera from el Port de Catarroja.
region_intro: Hiking around Catarroja is primarily defined by its proximity to the Albufera Natural Park, a significant wetland area. The region features expansive rice fields, marshes, and a diverse ecosystem, offering mostly flat terrain with minimal elevation changes. These landscapes provide unique opportunities for nature observation and walking, with trails often traversing agricultural land and wetlands. Best hiking trails around Catarroja The most popular hiking route is Port de Catarroja Rice Fields Loop, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This route offers direct views of the rice fields and wetlands from the Port de Catarroja. Another top favourite among local hikers is Closed of the Pipa circular from Catarroja — Parc natural de l'Albufera, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This trail explores the natural park's biodiversity and expansive wetland scenery. Local hikers also love the Old Devesa Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through the flat landscapes near Catarroja, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Catarroja is defined by its wetland ecosystems, extensive rice fields, and the flat terrain of the Albufera Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a significant number of easy routes. The routes in Catarroja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,000 reviews. More than 10,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Catarroja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Catarroja?
A: There are over 370 hiking routes available around Catarroja. The area is particularly known for its flat terrain and wetland ecosystems within the Albufera Natural Park, offering a wide range of options for nature observation.

Q: Are there many easy hiking trails in Catarroja?
A: Yes, Catarroja offers a significant number of easy hiking routes, with over 210 trails categorized as easy. These routes are generally flat, making them accessible for various fitness levels and ideal for enjoying the unique landscapes of the Albufera Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Catarroja?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14674989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pujol Pond – Pujol Lake loop from el Saler&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 11.8 km (7.3 miles) experience, allowing you to explore the natural beauty of the area and its wetlands.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Catarroja?
A: While many trails in natural parks generally allow dogs, it's always best to check specific regulations for the Albufera Natural Park, especially concerning protected areas and bird nesting seasons. Keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Catarroja?
A: Hiking in Catarroja primarily offers views of the Albufera Natural Park's unique wetland ecosystems. You'll encounter expansive rice fields, marshes, and a diverse array of birdlife. The terrain is largely flat, providing serene walks through agricultural land and natural habitats.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/404607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pujol Pond&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2439037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major attraction, known for its birdwatching opportunities and traditional 'Vela Latina' boats.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Catarroja?
A: The best time to hike in Catarroja, particularly within the Albufera Natural Park, is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. The rice fields offer a vibrant green landscape between May and September, which is also a great time for birdwatching. Winter can also be pleasant with cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hiking options in the wider Valencia province?
A: For those seeking longer treks, the Camino del Cid, a cultural-historical long-distance hiking route, passes through the wider Valencia province. The 'La Defensa del Sur' section offers a more challenging and extensive hiking experience, following the traces of the medieval knight Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Catarroja?
A: The hiking routes in Catarroja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,000 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique wetland scenery, the peaceful atmosphere of the rice fields, and the excellent birdwatching opportunities within the Albufera Natural Park.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking areas near Catarroja?
A: Catarroja's port serves as a main entry point to the Albufera Natural Park, and local public transport options are available to reach the town. From there, many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mural "The Port of Catarroja" – "Common Chaffinch" mural loop from Catarroja&lt;/a&gt;, are accessible directly from the town or its immediate surroundings.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Catarroja suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many trails in Catarroja are suitable for families, especially those within the Albufera Natural Park. The terrain is predominantly flat with minimal elevation changes, making it easy for children. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e827324756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Devesa Trail&lt;/a&gt; are easy and offer opportunities for nature observation, which can be engaging for younger hikers.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Catarroja?
A: Parking is generally available near the Port de Catarroja, which is a common starting point for many trails leading into the Albufera Natural Park. Specific parking information for individual trailheads can often be found on detailed route descriptions.
tips: Very picturesque and quiet site with the typical barges used for a long time. With a beautiful wooden walkway next to the ideal canal to walk an afternoon and watch the sunset

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Parque Natural de l'Albufera — Circular from el Port de Catarroja?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike typically takes about 1 hour and 15 minutes to 2 hours to complete. The route is approximately 5 kilometers long, making it a pleasant and manageable outing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. This route is categorized as an "easy hike" with minimal elevation gain, making it ideal for all fitness levels, including beginners and families with children. The paths are easily accessible and generally flat.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park at El Port de Catarroja?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from El Port de Catarroja. While specific parking details aren't provided, the area is generally accessible. For public transport options from Valencia, you can check local bus routes or visit the Visit Valencia website for up-to-date information on getting to L'Albufera Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly flat, traversing the northern marshland of L'Albufera. You'll walk through extensive rice fields and alongside canals and ditches that feed into the lake. The highest point is only about 8 meters above sea level, ensuring a very gentle walk.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the unique landscape of L'Albufera Natural Park, including vast rice fields that change color with the seasons, from vibrant green to golden. The route offers views of the freshwater lake, and it's a fantastic spot for birdwatching, with over 300 species like herons and flamingos. You might also spot traditional "barracas" and "alquerías" dotting the landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The park offers different beauty throughout the year. Between May and September, the rice fields are a vibrant green. Other seasons bring golden hues. For birdwatching, migratory seasons can be particularly rewarding. The lake is also famous for spectacular sunsets, making late afternoon a magical time to visit.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts from the Port of Catarroja. Beyond this, the main attractions are the natural beauty of the rice fields, the expansive lake, and the traditional Valencian architecture like barracas and alquerías that reflect the region's cultural history.

faqs.question: Does this circular route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of El Camí IT5TR6 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana, and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, as well as the Camí del Bony.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While L'Albufera Natural Park is a protected area, dogs are generally allowed on trails as long as they are kept on a leash and you clean up after them. Always respect local signage regarding pet regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for L'Albufera Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within L'Albufera Natural Park. It is a publicly accessible natural space, though it is important to respect the protected environment and its regulations.

faqs.question: What facilities are available near the trailhead at El Port de Catarroja?
faqs.answer: El Port de Catarroja and the surrounding villages, like El Palmar, are known for their local cuisine. You can expect to find cafes and restaurants, particularly those serving authentic Valencian paella, which originated in this region. Traditional boat trips on the lake are also a popular activity nearby.
